title: pulstran
summary: Generate pulse trains from named pulse functions, function handles, or sampled prototype pulses.

gpu_support.notes: GPU numeric inputs are gathered to the host because `pulstran` combines multiple shifted pulse evaluations and may call arbitrary pulse functions.

fusion.notes: `pulstran` materializes a full output vector and terminates fusion plans.

description: `pulstran(T, D, FUN, P1, ...)` evaluates a pulse function at `T - D(k)` for each delay and sums the results. `D` may be a delay vector or an N-by-2 delay/amplitude matrix. `pulstran(T, D, P, FS)` uses a sampled prototype pulse `P` with sample rate `FS` and linear interpolation.
behaviors: Character, string, and function-handle pulse names are accepted. Built-in fast paths cover `rectpuls`, `tripuls`, and `gauspuls`; other function handles are invoked through the normal `feval` path.
A delay vector uses unit amplitude for every pulse.
An N-by-2 delay matrix uses column one as delay and column two as amplitude.
Sampled prototype pulses are linearly interpolated at `FS`; samples outside the prototype support contribute zero.
Complex and text-valued numeric inputs are rejected with builtin-scoped errors.

faqs.question: Which pulse functions are recognized directly?
faqs.answer: `rectpuls`, `tripuls`, and `gauspuls` have direct built-in paths. Other function handles are dispatched through RunMat's regular function-handle mechanism.

faqs.question: Does `pulstran` preserve the orientation of `T`?
faqs.answer: Yes. The output tensor uses the same shape as `T`, including row-vector and column-vector orientation.
